173            # => user's responsibility: ensure that the pandas.Dataframe
174            #    contains just two columns: X-values and Y-values
175            #    (for example, pass something like: df[['Pixel','Iraw']]
176            # * we use type(input_data)==pd.DataFrame instead of isinstance
177            #   we need to do this due the the next option = {ediff.io.Profile}
178            #   => {ediff.io.Profile} is-instance of pd.DataFrame
179            #   => we would think that {Profiles} are DataFrames 
180            name = 'pd.DataFrame'
181            data = np.transpose(np.array(input_data))
182            diff1D = None
183        elif input_data.__class__.__name__ == 'Diffractogram1D':
184            # The input is ediff.io.Profile.
185            # (special case, input from our own, connected package ediff
186            # => convert Profile to numpy.array
187            # => the Profile has more-or-less fixed structure
188            #    the array is created from Profile columns {Pixel} a {Iraw}
189            # * we use var.__class__.__name__ instead of type
190            #   we DO NOT KNOW ediff.io.Profile here - it is not imported
191            #   we could import this, but then bground would depend on ediff
192            name = 'ediff.io.Diffractogram1D'
193            data = np.transpose(np.array(input_data[['Pixels','Iraw']]))
194            diff1D = input_data
195        else:
196            raise TypeError('Unknown type of input data!')
197         
198        # Return the result = 2xN numpy array with XY-data.
199        # (i.e. data[0] = X-data/values, data[1] = Y-data/values)
200        return(name, data, diff1D)

Class defining {InputData} for background subtraction.

  • Input data can be: filename, np.array, pd.DataFrame, or ediff.io.Diffractogram1D object.
  • In any case, the input data should contain two columns: [X, Y = Iraw = raw_intensity].
InputData(input_data, **kwargs) 
 64    def __init__(self, input_data, **kwargs):
 65        '''
 66        Initialize {InputData} object that defines input for bkg subtraction.
 67
 68        Parameters
 69        ----------
 70        input_data : str or np.array or pd.DataFrame or ediff.io.Diffraction1D
 71            The input_data are converted to
 72            self.name, self.data and self.diff1D sub-objects.
 73            Self.name = name of the input file (or description of the source).
 74            Self.data = np.array with 2 rows: `[X, Y = Iraw = raw intensity]`.
 75            Self.diff1D = None or ediff.io.Diffraction1D object
 76            if it was given as an argument.
 77        kwargs : keyword arguments for pd.read_csv, optional
 78            The keyword arguments are relevant only if {input_data}
 79            is str/PathLike object defining the input data file.
 80            The keyword arguments are passed to pd.read_csv function
 81            to read the file as a simple DataFrame, which is immediately
 82            converted to a simple np.array = to self.data sub-object
 83            described above.
 84
 85        Returns
 86        -------
 87        bground.api.InputData
 88            The object is fully initialized
 89            and ready to use in background subtraction methods.
 90            It contains the self.name,
 91            self.data and (optionally) self.diff1D sub-objects described above.
 92            
 93        Technical notes
 94        ---------------
 95        * If {input_data} is a file, we read it to np.array with
 96          pd.read_csv instead of np.loadtxt, as it is more flexible/powerful.
 97        * The {kwargs} are pre-set reasonably enough to read most of common
 98          files, but user can adjuste them if needed - of course.
 99        * If {kwargs} need to be adjusted, see the comments in the source
100          code and pandas.read_csv online documentation.
101        '''
102        
103        # Initialization of InputData object.
104        # * Two pieces of input:
105        #   input_data (file or array or DataFrame or ediff.io.Diffraction1D)
106        #   kwargs (to read the file using pd.read_csv function
107        # * The input is saved in two/three properties:
108        #   self.name = name of the input file (or str describing data source)
109        #   self.data = the XY-data itself = array with 2 rows [X,Y=Intensity]
110        #   self.diff1D = extra object if ediff.io.Diffracton1D was the input
111        #                 this is set to None if the input was "common data"
112        self.name, self.data, self.diff1D = \
113            InputData.read_input_data(input_data, **kwargs)

Initialize {InputData} object that defines input for bkg subtraction.

Parameters
  • input_data (str or np.array or pd.DataFrame or ediff.io.Diffraction1D): The input_data are converted to self.name, self.data and self.diff1D sub-objects. Self.name = name of the input file (or description of the source). Self.data = np.array with 2 rows: [X, Y = Iraw = raw intensity]. Self.diff1D = None or ediff.io.Diffraction1D object if it was given as an argument.
  • kwargs (keyword arguments for pd.read_csv, optional): The keyword arguments are relevant only if {input_data} is str/PathLike object defining the input data file. The keyword arguments are passed to pd.read_csv function to read the file as a simple DataFrame, which is immediately converted to a simple np.array = to self.data sub-object described above.
Returns
  • bground.api.InputData: The object is fully initialized and ready to use in background subtraction methods. It contains the self.name, self.data and (optionally) self.diff1D sub-objects described above.
Technical notes
  • If {input_data} is a file, we read it to np.array with pd.read_csv instead of np.loadtxt, as it is more flexible/powerful.
  • The {kwargs} are pre-set reasonably enough to read most of common files, but user can adjuste them if needed - of course.
  • If {kwargs} need to be adjusted, see the comments in the source code and pandas.read_csv online documentation.
@staticmethod
def read_input_data(input_data, **kwargs): 
116    @staticmethod
117    def read_input_data(input_data, **kwargs):
118        '''
119        Read {input_data} using optional {kwargs}
120        return the results to be saved in {InputData} object properties.
121    
122        * intrinsic method of {InputData} object
123        * more info => look at the detailed comments in the func source code
124        '''
125        # Read input data with XY-data.
126        # No docstring: just class description above + comments below.
127        # * The optional arguments (comment, sep, usecols)
128        #   are passed to pandas.read_csv if the input_data ~ filename.
129    
130        if isinstance(input_data, (str, Path)):
131            # The input is a file = filename given be str or PathLike var.
132            # => read the file using pd.read_csv
133            # => user's responsibility: modify optional args as needed
134            #    the optional arguments are: comment, sep, usecols
135            #    these arguments are passed to pd.read_csv
136            # (a) set name = filename
137            name = str(input_data)
138            # (b) get **kwargs for pd.read_csv OR set reasonable defaults
139            # ... comments may be present
140            my_comment  = kwargs.get('comment') or '#'
141            # ... header should be None if there are no column headers!
142            my_header   = kwargs.get('header') or 'infer'
143            # ... skiprows can be defined for completness and flexibility
144            my_skiprows = kwargs.get('skiprows') or 0
145            # ... separator can be defined, defalt is any whitespace
146            my_sep      = kwargs.get('sep') or r'\s+'
147            # ... usecols may be defined, default is the first two columns
148            my_usecols  = kwargs.get('usecols') or [0,1]
149            # ... columns may be re-defined OR default column names are used
150            my_columns  = kwargs.get('columns') or None
151            # (c) read the file with pd.read_csv and the **kwargs from above 
152            df = pd.read_csv(input_data, 
153                comment=my_comment, header=my_header, skiprows=my_skiprows, 
154                sep=my_sep, usecols=my_usecols)
155            if my_columns is not None: df.columns = my_columns
156            # (d) convert pd.DataFrame to np.ndarray
157            #     and transpose the array (columns should be the 1st index)
158            data = np.transpose(np.array(df))
159            # (e) set that the data are NOT the special ed.io.Profile
160            diff1D = None
161        elif isinstance(input_data, np.ndarray):
162            # The input is a numpy array.
163            # => just assign array to data
164            # => user's responsibility: ensure that the numpy.array
165            #    contains just two rows: X-values and Y-values
166            #    (for example, pass something like: arr[[0,2]]
167            name = 'np.ndarray'
168            data = input_data
169            diff1D = None
170        elif type(input_data) == pd.DataFrame:
171            # The input is pandas.DataFrame
172            # => convert DataFrame to numpy.array
173            # => user's responsibility: ensure that the pandas.Dataframe
174            #    contains just two columns: X-values and Y-values
175            #    (for example, pass something like: df[['Pixel','Iraw']]
176            # * we use type(input_data)==pd.DataFrame instead of isinstance
177            #   we need to do this due the the next option = {ediff.io.Profile}
178            #   => {ediff.io.Profile} is-instance of pd.DataFrame
179            #   => we would think that {Profiles} are DataFrames 
180            name = 'pd.DataFrame'
181            data = np.transpose(np.array(input_data))
182            diff1D = None
183        elif input_data.__class__.__name__ == 'Diffractogram1D':
184            # The input is ediff.io.Profile.
185            # (special case, input from our own, connected package ediff
186            # => convert Profile to numpy.array
187            # => the Profile has more-or-less fixed structure
188            #    the array is created from Profile columns {Pixel} a {Iraw}
189            # * we use var.__class__.__name__ instead of type
190            #   we DO NOT KNOW ediff.io.Profile here - it is not imported
191            #   we could import this, but then bground would depend on ediff
192            name = 'ediff.io.Diffractogram1D'
193            data = np.transpose(np.array(input_data[['Pixels','Iraw']]))
194            diff1D = input_data
195        else:
196            raise TypeError('Unknown type of input data!')
197         
198        # Return the result = 2xN numpy array with XY-data.
199        # (i.e. data[0] = X-data/values, data[1] = Y-data/values)
200        return(name, data, diff1D)

Read {input_data} using optional {kwargs} return the results to be saved in {InputData} object properties.

  • intrinsic method of {InputData} object
  • more info => look at the detailed comments in the func source code
class BkgParams: 
203class BkgParams:
204    '''
205    Class defining {BkgParams} = background parameters
206    for background subtraction.
207    
208    * BkgParams, group 1 = name of the output/background file(s).
209    * BkgParams, group 2 = x,y-labels and x,y-limits of the interactive plot
210    * BkgParams, group 3 = messages argument;
211      if True the program prints brief messages on stdout. 
212    '''
213    
214    def __init__(self, bkg_file=None, 
215                 xlabel=None, ylabel=None, xlim=None, ylim=None, 
216                 saveTXT=True, messages=True):
217        '''
218        Initialize {BkgParams} object that defines properties of background.
219
220        Parameters
221        ----------
222        bkg_file : str or PathLike object
223            Name of the background/output file(s).
224            There can be three different output files:
225            data file (XY-data, basic output; {bkg_file}.txt),
226            background points (if bkg points are defined; {bkg_file}.txt.bp),
227            background plot (if user requests this; {bkg_file}.txt.png).
228        xlabel : str, optional, default is None
229            Label of X-axis (in the background plots).
230        ylabel : str, optional, default is None
231            Label of X-axis (in the background plots).
232        xlim : float or tuple of two floats, optional, default is None
233            X-range (in the background plots).
234            If xlim = 300, set Xrange/xlim to (0,300).
235            If xlim = (50,300), set Xrange/xlim to (50,300).
236        ylim : float or tuple of two floats, optional, default is None
237            Y-range (in the background plots).
238            If ylim = 300, set Yrange/ylim to (0,300).
239            If ylim = (50,300), set Yrange/ylim to (50,300).
240        saveTXT : bool, optional, default is True
241            If True (default), save also in TXT and TXT.BP files.
242            If False, the results are saved only within this object.
243            The False in suitable for certain automated processing paths.            
244        messages : bool, optional, default is True
245            If True, the program shows short messages on stdout as it runs.
246
247        Returns
248        -------
249        bground.api.BkgParams object
250            The object is fully initialized and ready to be used.
251            In fact the object is simple
252            - just a collectoin of properties, no methods.
253        '''
254        # Initialization of BkgParams object => 3 groups of params:
255        #  1) self.bkg_file = name of background file(s)
256        #  2) self.xlabel/ylabel/xlim/ylim = params of bkg plots
257        #  3) self.saveTXT  = if True (default), save results in TXT-files
258        #     self.messages = if True (default), print short messages on stdout
259        #------
260        
261        # (1) Set the name of background/output file(s), which can be:
262        # * some.txt     = main output, 4 cols = [X, Iraw, Ibkg, I=(Iraw-Ibkg)]
263        # * some.txt.bp  = saved background points (for possible recalculation)
264        # * some.txt.png = saved plot (from interactive matplotlib interface)
265        #------
266        if bkg_file is None:
267            # In some cases, we may not want to save output to TXT-files.
268            # => then we save the data just in the calling object:
269            # => specifically in: self.data, self.background, and self.diff1D.
270            self.bkg_file = None
271        else:
272            # In classical usage, we save the data also in output files, 
273            # If {bkg_file} is a PathLike object, convert to string
274            bkg_file = str(bkg_file)
275            # If {bkg_file} ends with '.bp' or '.txt', remove the extension
276            # (this may be a copy paste error
277            # (OR intentional in the inherited method RestoreFromBackground,
278            # (where the input bkg_file usually HAS the extension '.txt.bp'
279            bkg_file = bkg_file.lower()
280            bkg_file = bkg_file.removesuffix('.bp')
281            bkg_file = bkg_file.removesuffix('.png')
282            # Ii the out_file name does not have .txt extension, add
283            if not(bkg_file.lower().endswith('.txt')):
284                bkg_file = bkg_file + '.txt'
285            # Save the final name to self.out_file
286            self.bkg_file = bkg_file
287        
288        # (2) Set the interactive plot parameters
289        # (x,y-labels
290        self.xlabel = xlabel            # x-axis label of the interactive plot
291        self.ylabel = ylabel            # y-axis label of the interactive plot
292        # (x,y-limits
293        if isinstance(xlim,list): self.xlim = xlim  # xlim = [xmin,xmax]
294        else: self.xlim = [0,xlim]                  # ...or just xmax
295        if isinstance(ylim,list): self.ylim = ylim  # ylim = [ymin,ymax]
296        else: self.ylim = [0,ylim]                  # ...or just ymax
297        
298        # (3) Additional parameters
299        # saveTXT - if {True} save results also TXT and TXT.BP files
300        # messages - if {True} print brief messages to stdout
301        self.saveTXT = saveTXT
302        self.messages = messages

Class defining {BkgParams} = background parameters for background subtraction.

  • BkgParams, group 1 = name of the output/background file(s).
  • BkgParams, group 2 = x,y-labels and x,y-limits of the interactive plot
  • BkgParams, group 3 = messages argument; if True the program prints brief messages on stdout.
BkgParams( bkg_file=None, xlabel=None, ylabel=None, xlim=None, ylim=None, saveTXT=True, messages=True) 
214    def __init__(self, bkg_file=None, 
215                 xlabel=None, ylabel=None, xlim=None, ylim=None, 
216                 saveTXT=True, messages=True):
217        '''
218        Initialize {BkgParams} object that defines properties of background.
219
220        Parameters
221        ----------
222        bkg_file : str or PathLike object
223            Name of the background/output file(s).
224            There can be three different output files:
225            data file (XY-data, basic output; {bkg_file}.txt),
226            background points (if bkg points are defined; {bkg_file}.txt.bp),
227            background plot (if user requests this; {bkg_file}.txt.png).
228        xlabel : str, optional, default is None
229            Label of X-axis (in the background plots).
230        ylabel : str, optional, default is None
231            Label of X-axis (in the background plots).
232        xlim : float or tuple of two floats, optional, default is None
233            X-range (in the background plots).
234            If xlim = 300, set Xrange/xlim to (0,300).
235            If xlim = (50,300), set Xrange/xlim to (50,300).
236        ylim : float or tuple of two floats, optional, default is None
237            Y-range (in the background plots).
238            If ylim = 300, set Yrange/ylim to (0,300).
239            If ylim = (50,300), set Yrange/ylim to (50,300).
240        saveTXT : bool, optional, default is True
241            If True (default), save also in TXT and TXT.BP files.
242            If False, the results are saved only within this object.
243            The False in suitable for certain automated processing paths.            
244        messages : bool, optional, default is True
245            If True, the program shows short messages on stdout as it runs.
246
247        Returns
248        -------
249        bground.api.BkgParams object
250            The object is fully initialized and ready to be used.
251            In fact the object is simple
252            - just a collectoin of properties, no methods.
253        '''
254        # Initialization of BkgParams object => 3 groups of params:
255        #  1) self.bkg_file = name of background file(s)
256        #  2) self.xlabel/ylabel/xlim/ylim = params of bkg plots
257        #  3) self.saveTXT  = if True (default), save results in TXT-files
258        #     self.messages = if True (default), print short messages on stdout
259        #------
260        
261        # (1) Set the name of background/output file(s), which can be:
262        # * some.txt     = main output, 4 cols = [X, Iraw, Ibkg, I=(Iraw-Ibkg)]
263        # * some.txt.bp  = saved background points (for possible recalculation)
264        # * some.txt.png = saved plot (from interactive matplotlib interface)
265        #------
266        if bkg_file is None:
267            # In some cases, we may not want to save output to TXT-files.
268            # => then we save the data just in the calling object:
269            # => specifically in: self.data, self.background, and self.diff1D.
270            self.bkg_file = None
271        else:
272            # In classical usage, we save the data also in output files, 
273            # If {bkg_file} is a PathLike object, convert to string
274            bkg_file = str(bkg_file)
275            # If {bkg_file} ends with '.bp' or '.txt', remove the extension
276            # (this may be a copy paste error
277            # (OR intentional in the inherited method RestoreFromBackground,
278            # (where the input bkg_file usually HAS the extension '.txt.bp'
279            bkg_file = bkg_file.lower()
280            bkg_file = bkg_file.removesuffix('.bp')
281            bkg_file = bkg_file.removesuffix('.png')
282            # Ii the out_file name does not have .txt extension, add
283            if not(bkg_file.lower().endswith('.txt')):
284                bkg_file = bkg_file + '.txt'
285            # Save the final name to self.out_file
286            self.bkg_file = bkg_file
287        
288        # (2) Set the interactive plot parameters
289        # (x,y-labels
290        self.xlabel = xlabel            # x-axis label of the interactive plot
291        self.ylabel = ylabel            # y-axis label of the interactive plot
292        # (x,y-limits
293        if isinstance(xlim,list): self.xlim = xlim  # xlim = [xmin,xmax]
294        else: self.xlim = [0,xlim]                  # ...or just xmax
295        if isinstance(ylim,list): self.ylim = ylim  # ylim = [ymin,ymax]
296        else: self.ylim = [0,ylim]                  # ...or just ymax
297        
298        # (3) Additional parameters
299        # saveTXT - if {True} save results also TXT and TXT.BP files
300        # messages - if {True} print brief messages to stdout
301        self.saveTXT = saveTXT
302        self.messages = messages

Initialize {BkgParams} object that defines properties of background.

Parameters
  • bkg_file (str or PathLike object): Name of the background/output file(s). There can be three different output files: data file (XY-data, basic output; {bkg_file}.txt), background points (if bkg points are defined; {bkg_file}.txt.bp), background plot (if user requests this; {bkg_file}.txt.png).
  • xlabel (str, optional, default is None): Label of X-axis (in the background plots).
  • ylabel (str, optional, default is None): Label of X-axis (in the background plots).
  • xlim (float or tuple of two floats, optional, default is None): X-range (in the background plots). If xlim = 300, set Xrange/xlim to (0,300). If xlim = (50,300), set Xrange/xlim to (50,300).
  • ylim (float or tuple of two floats, optional, default is None): Y-range (in the background plots). If ylim = 300, set Yrange/ylim to (0,300). If ylim = (50,300), set Yrange/ylim to (50,300).
  • saveTXT (bool, optional, default is True): If True (default), save also in TXT and TXT.BP files. If False, the results are saved only within this object. The False in suitable for certain automated processing paths.
  • messages (bool, optional, default is True): If True, the program shows short messages on stdout as it runs.
Returns
  • bground.api.BkgParams object: The object is fully initialized and ready to be used. In fact the object is simple
    • just a collectoin of properties, no methods.
class InteractivePlot: 
305class InteractivePlot:
306    '''
307    Class defining {InteractivePlot} method of backround subtraction.
308    
309    * When running the method, a new window with interactive plot is opened.
310    * The user defines background points using mouse and keyboard shortcuts.
311    * The program does the rest - it subtracts bkg + shows/saves the results.
312    
313    The method can be run in two ways:
314        
315    * Classical, step-by-step approach - see the example below
316    * Modern, single-funtion approach - see bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot
317    
318    Example - running InteractivePlot in classical way:
319        
320    >>> # Standard import
321    >>> # (alternative: import ediff as ed => bkg is available as ed.bkg
322    >>> import bground.api as bkg
323    >>>
324    >>> # Define I/O files
325    >>> # (input file  = XYdata, 2 cols: [X, Y = Iraw = Raw Intensity]
326    >>> # (output file = XYdata, 4 cols: [X, Iraw, Inet, I = (Iraw - Inet)]
327    >>> IN  = 'data1_raw_intensity.txt'
328    >>> OUT = 'data2_bkg_subtracted.txt'
329    >>>
330    >>> # Define auxiliary objects
331    >>> DATA = bkg.InputData(IN, usecols=[0,1], unpack=True)
332    >>> PPAR = bkg.BkgParams(OUT,'Pix','Intensity',xlim=[0,200],ylim=[0,180])
333    >>>
334    >>> # Initialize and run InteractivePlot subtraction method
335    >>> # (the method is initialized using the auxiliary objects and run
336    >>> # (SMET.data + SMET.background sub-objects will contain the results
337    >>> SMET = bkg.InteractivePlot(DATA, PPAR)
338    >>> SMET.run()
339    '''
340    
341    
342    def __init__(self, DATA, PARS, CLI=False):
343        '''
344        Initialize {InteractivePlot} object.
345
346        Parameters
347        ----------
348        DATA : bground.api.InputData object
349            The object contains description of input data.
350            The {InputData} object should be defined
351            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
352            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
353        PARS : bground.api.BkgParams object
354            The object contains description of input data.
355            The {BkgData} object should be defined
356            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
357            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
358        CLI : bool, optional, default is False
359            If we run the {InteractivePLot} from CLI interface,
360            this arbument should be set to True so that the plot stayed
361            in the screen.
362
363        Returns
364        -------
365        bground.api.InteractivePlot object
366            The object should be ready to use.
367            The principal object method is InteractivePlot.run.
368        '''
369        
370        # Save properties from DATA object
371        self.name = DATA.name
372        self.data = DATA.data
373        self.diff1D = DATA.diff1D
374        
375        # Save properties from PARS object
376        self.pars = PARS
377        
378        # Additional property - empty XYbackground object
379        # (this object is defined as a semi-empty object here
380        # (the only argument we supply is the name of the background file
381        self.background = \
382            bground.points.bdata.XYbackground(self.pars.bkg_file)
383            
384        # Initialize plotting - by means of composition
385        self.plots = Plots(self)
386        
387        # Initialize specific interactive backend
388        # in case Python runs in CLI = Command Line Interface,
389        # i.e. if the program runs outside Spyder or Jupyter environments
390        if CLI == True:
391            matplotlib.use('QtAgg')
392
393        
394    def run(self):
395        '''
396        Run {InteractivePlot} method of background subtraction.
397        
398        * The method (i) clears variables
399          and (ii) shows the plot on the screen.
400        * The plot is waiting for the user to define
401          background points with keyboard and mouse. 
402        * A brief help is printed on stdout
403          when the interactive plot is opened.
404        * No params; everything defined during InteractivePlot initialization.
405        '''
406
407        # (0) Clear background points from possible previous runs.
408        # * Issue in Jupyter, when re-running the cell with this command
409        self.background.points.X = []
410        self.background.points.Y = []
411        
412        # (1) Run the interactive plot
413        # * the plot appears in a new window
414        #   if we use the recommended %matplotlib qt
415        # * in addition, a brief help is printed to to stdout
416        #   = Screen in Python, Console in Spyder, output cell in Jupyter
417        # * the return values seem to be necessary for current Jupyter version
418        fig,ax = bground.points.ifunc.interactive_plot(self)
419        
420        # (2) Optimize the layout of the returned figure and show the plot.
421        # * fig.tight_layout() functin works
422        #   in all three supported interfaces: CLI, Spyder, Jupyter
423        fig.tight_layout()
424        plt.show()

Class defining {InteractivePlot} method of backround subtraction.

  • When running the method, a new window with interactive plot is opened.
  • The user defines background points using mouse and keyboard shortcuts.
  • The program does the rest - it subtracts bkg + shows/saves the results.

The method can be run in two ways:

  • Classical, step-by-step approach - see the example below
  • Modern, single-funtion approach - see bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot

Example - running InteractivePlot in classical way:

>>> # Standard import
>>> # (alternative: import ediff as ed => bkg is available as ed.bkg
>>> import bground.api as bkg
>>>
>>> # Define I/O files
>>> # (input file  = XYdata, 2 cols: [X, Y = Iraw = Raw Intensity]
>>> # (output file = XYdata, 4 cols: [X, Iraw, Inet, I = (Iraw - Inet)]
>>> IN  = 'data1_raw_intensity.txt'
>>> OUT = 'data2_bkg_subtracted.txt'
>>>
>>> # Define auxiliary objects
>>> DATA = bkg.InputData(IN, usecols=[0,1], unpack=True)
>>> PPAR = bkg.BkgParams(OUT,'Pix','Intensity',xlim=[0,200],ylim=[0,180])
>>>
>>> # Initialize and run InteractivePlot subtraction method
>>> # (the method is initialized using the auxiliary objects and run
>>> # (SMET.data + SMET.background sub-objects will contain the results
>>> SMET = bkg.InteractivePlot(DATA, PPAR)
>>> SMET.run()
InteractivePlot(DATA, PARS, CLI=False) 
342    def __init__(self, DATA, PARS, CLI=False):
343        '''
344        Initialize {InteractivePlot} object.
345
346        Parameters
347        ----------
348        DATA : bground.api.InputData object
349            The object contains description of input data.
350            The {InputData} object should be defined
351            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
352            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
353        PARS : bground.api.BkgParams object
354            The object contains description of input data.
355            The {BkgData} object should be defined
356            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
357            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
358        CLI : bool, optional, default is False
359            If we run the {InteractivePLot} from CLI interface,
360            this arbument should be set to True so that the plot stayed
361            in the screen.
362
363        Returns
364        -------
365        bground.api.InteractivePlot object
366            The object should be ready to use.
367            The principal object method is InteractivePlot.run.
368        '''
369        
370        # Save properties from DATA object
371        self.name = DATA.name
372        self.data = DATA.data
373        self.diff1D = DATA.diff1D
374        
375        # Save properties from PARS object
376        self.pars = PARS
377        
378        # Additional property - empty XYbackground object
379        # (this object is defined as a semi-empty object here
380        # (the only argument we supply is the name of the background file
381        self.background = \
382            bground.points.bdata.XYbackground(self.pars.bkg_file)
383            
384        # Initialize plotting - by means of composition
385        self.plots = Plots(self)
386        
387        # Initialize specific interactive backend
388        # in case Python runs in CLI = Command Line Interface,
389        # i.e. if the program runs outside Spyder or Jupyter environments
390        if CLI == True:
391            matplotlib.use('QtAgg')

Initialize {InteractivePlot} object.

Parameters
  • DATA (bground.api.InputData object): The object contains description of input data. The {InputData} object should be defined either before initializing the {InteractivePlot}, or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
  • PARS (bground.api.BkgParams object): The object contains description of input data. The {BkgData} object should be defined either before initializing the {InteractivePlot}, or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
  • CLI (bool, optional, default is False): If we run the {InteractivePLot} from CLI interface, this arbument should be set to True so that the plot stayed in the screen.
Returns
  • bground.api.InteractivePlot object: The object should be ready to use. The principal object method is InteractivePlot.run.
def run(self): 
394    def run(self):
395        '''
396        Run {InteractivePlot} method of background subtraction.
397        
398        * The method (i) clears variables
399          and (ii) shows the plot on the screen.
400        * The plot is waiting for the user to define
401          background points with keyboard and mouse. 
402        * A brief help is printed on stdout
403          when the interactive plot is opened.
404        * No params; everything defined during InteractivePlot initialization.
405        '''
406
407        # (0) Clear background points from possible previous runs.
408        # * Issue in Jupyter, when re-running the cell with this command
409        self.background.points.X = []
410        self.background.points.Y = []
411        
412        # (1) Run the interactive plot
413        # * the plot appears in a new window
414        #   if we use the recommended %matplotlib qt
415        # * in addition, a brief help is printed to to stdout
416        #   = Screen in Python, Console in Spyder, output cell in Jupyter
417        # * the return values seem to be necessary for current Jupyter version
418        fig,ax = bground.points.ifunc.interactive_plot(self)
419        
420        # (2) Optimize the layout of the returned figure and show the plot.
421        # * fig.tight_layout() functin works
422        #   in all three supported interfaces: CLI, Spyder, Jupyter
423        fig.tight_layout()
424        plt.show()

Run {InteractivePlot} method of background subtraction.

  • The method (i) clears variables and (ii) shows the plot on the screen.
  • The plot is waiting for the user to define background points with keyboard and mouse.
  • A brief help is printed on stdout when the interactive plot is opened.
  • No params; everything defined during InteractivePlot initialization.
class RestoreFromPoints(InteractivePlot): 
427class RestoreFromPoints(InteractivePlot):
428    '''
429    RestoreFromPoints background subtraction method.
430    
431    * This is a specific case (and subclass) of InteractivePlot method.
432    * It inherits the initialization and visualization from InteractivePlot.
433    * But it runs non-interactively, just reading bkgpoints + calculating bkg.
434    
435    The method can be run in two ways:
436        
437    * Classical, step-by-step approach - see the example below
438    * Modern, single-funtion approach - see bground.api.Run.RestoreFromPoints
439    
440    Example - running RestoreFromPoints in classical way:
441        
442    >>> # Standard import
443    >>> # (alternative: import ediff as ed => bkg is available as ed.bkg
444    >>> import bground.api as bkg
445    >>>
446    >>> # Define I/O files
447    >>> IN  = 'data1_raw_intensity.txt'
448    >>> BKG = 'data2_bkg_subtracted.txt.bp'
449    >>>
450    >>> # Define auxiliary objects
451    >>> DATA = bkg.InputData(IN, usecols=[0,1], unpack=True)
452    >>> PPAR = bkg.BkgParams(BKG,'Pix','Intensity',xlim=[0,200],ylim=[0,180])
453    >>>
454    >>> # Initialize and run RestoreFromPoints subtraction method
455    >>> # (SMET.data + SMET.background sub-objects will contain the results
456    >>> SMET = bkg.RestoreFromPoints(DATA, PPAR)
457    >>> SMET.run()
458    '''
459    
460    def __init__(self, DATA, PARS, CLI=False):
461        '''
462        Initialize {RestoreFromPoints} object.
463
464        Parameters
465        ----------
466        DATA : bground.api.InputData object
467            The object contains description of input data.
468            The {InputData} object should be defined
469            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
470            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
471        PARS : bground.api.BkgParams object
472            The object contains description of input data.
473            The {BkgData} object should be defined
474            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
475            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
476        CLI : bool, optional, default is False
477            If we run the {InteractivePLot} from CLI interface,
478            this arbument should be set to True so that the plot stayed
479            in the screen.
480
481        Returns
482        -------
483        bground.api.RestoreFromPoints object
484            The object should be ready to use.
485            The principal object method is InteractivePlot.run.
486        '''
487        
488        # The same initialization like in the superclass.
489        # (if __init__ contains just this command, it can be ommited
490        # (here: just for historical reasons; we used to have additional args
491        super().__init__(DATA, PARS, CLI)
492        
493        # Note: it is possible to initialize this function
494        # with all possible names: 'some', 'some.txt', 'some.txt.bp'
495        # and the initialization procedure of BkgParams calls resolves this.
496        
497        # Note: CLI=True if program runs from pure Python in command line
498        # => in super().__init__ we set the same matplotlib backend
499        # => we get the same interface in InteractivePlot and RestoreFromPoints
500        # => for compatibility with methods show_data_with_bkg_definition ...
501 
502
503    def run(self):
504        '''
505        Run {RestoreFromPoints} method of background subtraction.
506        
507        * The method reads a TXT.BP file with background points
508          and recalculates background and saves the results.
509        * The TXT.BP file usually comes from
510          a previous run of InteractivePlot method.
511        * No params; everything defined during InteractivePlot initialization.
512        '''
513
514        # (0) Read background points
515        # (self.background comes from initialization
516        # (it contains the name of the file with bkg points
517        # (this filename is in PARS and saved in self.background.bname
518        self.background = bground.points.bfunc.load_bkg_points(self)
519        
520        # (1) Calculate background-corrected data AND save them to self.data
521        # (self.data = the RestoreFromPoints object ALWAYS contains the output
522        self.data = bground.points.bfunc.calculate_bkg_data(self)
523        
524        # (2) Save the calculated data ALSO in self.diff1D => if it was defined
525        # (self.diff1D IF ediff.io.Diffractogram1D used for the initialization 
526        if self.diff1D is not None:
527            self.diff1D['Ibkg'] = self.data[2]
528            self.diff1D['I']    = self.data[3]
529
530        # (3) Save the calculated data ALSO to out_file => if it was defined
531        # (save data to file IF {saveTXT} is True
532        if self.pars.saveTXT is True:
533            bground.points.bfunc.save_bkg_data(self)

RestoreFromPoints background subtraction method.

  • This is a specific case (and subclass) of InteractivePlot method.
  • It inherits the initialization and visualization from InteractivePlot.
  • But it runs non-interactively, just reading bkgpoints + calculating bkg.

The method can be run in two ways:

  • Classical, step-by-step approach - see the example below
  • Modern, single-funtion approach - see bground.api.Run.RestoreFromPoints

Example - running RestoreFromPoints in classical way:

>>> # Standard import
>>> # (alternative: import ediff as ed => bkg is available as ed.bkg
>>> import bground.api as bkg
>>>
>>> # Define I/O files
>>> IN  = 'data1_raw_intensity.txt'
>>> BKG = 'data2_bkg_subtracted.txt.bp'
>>>
>>> # Define auxiliary objects
>>> DATA = bkg.InputData(IN, usecols=[0,1], unpack=True)
>>> PPAR = bkg.BkgParams(BKG,'Pix','Intensity',xlim=[0,200],ylim=[0,180])
>>>
>>> # Initialize and run RestoreFromPoints subtraction method
>>> # (SMET.data + SMET.background sub-objects will contain the results
>>> SMET = bkg.RestoreFromPoints(DATA, PPAR)
>>> SMET.run()
RestoreFromPoints(DATA, PARS, CLI=False) 
460    def __init__(self, DATA, PARS, CLI=False):
461        '''
462        Initialize {RestoreFromPoints} object.
463
464        Parameters
465        ----------
466        DATA : bground.api.InputData object
467            The object contains description of input data.
468            The {InputData} object should be defined
469            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
470            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
471        PARS : bground.api.BkgParams object
472            The object contains description of input data.
473            The {BkgData} object should be defined
474            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
475            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
476        CLI : bool, optional, default is False
477            If we run the {InteractivePLot} from CLI interface,
478            this arbument should be set to True so that the plot stayed
479            in the screen.
480
481        Returns
482        -------
483        bground.api.RestoreFromPoints object
484            The object should be ready to use.
485            The principal object method is InteractivePlot.run.
486        '''
487        
488        # The same initialization like in the superclass.
489        # (if __init__ contains just this command, it can be ommited
490        # (here: just for historical reasons; we used to have additional args
491        super().__init__(DATA, PARS, CLI)
492        
493        # Note: it is possible to initialize this function
494        # with all possible names: 'some', 'some.txt', 'some.txt.bp'
495        # and the initialization procedure of BkgParams calls resolves this.
496        
497        # Note: CLI=True if program runs from pure Python in command line
498        # => in super().__init__ we set the same matplotlib backend
499        # => we get the same interface in InteractivePlot and RestoreFromPoints
500        # => for compatibility with methods show_data_with_bkg_definition ...

Initialize {RestoreFromPoints} object.

Parameters
  • DATA (bground.api.InputData object): The object contains description of input data. The {InputData} object should be defined either before initializing the {InteractivePlot}, or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
  • PARS (bground.api.BkgParams object): The object contains description of input data. The {BkgData} object should be defined either before initializing the {InteractivePlot}, or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
  • CLI (bool, optional, default is False): If we run the {InteractivePLot} from CLI interface, this arbument should be set to True so that the plot stayed in the screen.
Returns
  • bground.api.RestoreFromPoints object: The object should be ready to use. The principal object method is InteractivePlot.run.
def run(self): 
503    def run(self):
504        '''
505        Run {RestoreFromPoints} method of background subtraction.
506        
507        * The method reads a TXT.BP file with background points
508          and recalculates background and saves the results.
509        * The TXT.BP file usually comes from
510          a previous run of InteractivePlot method.
511        * No params; everything defined during InteractivePlot initialization.
512        '''
513
514        # (0) Read background points
515        # (self.background comes from initialization
516        # (it contains the name of the file with bkg points
517        # (this filename is in PARS and saved in self.background.bname
518        self.background = bground.points.bfunc.load_bkg_points(self)
519        
520        # (1) Calculate background-corrected data AND save them to self.data
521        # (self.data = the RestoreFromPoints object ALWAYS contains the output
522        self.data = bground.points.bfunc.calculate_bkg_data(self)
523        
524        # (2) Save the calculated data ALSO in self.diff1D => if it was defined
525        # (self.diff1D IF ediff.io.Diffractogram1D used for the initialization 
526        if self.diff1D is not None:
527            self.diff1D['Ibkg'] = self.data[2]
528            self.diff1D['I']    = self.data[3]
529
530        # (3) Save the calculated data ALSO to out_file => if it was defined
531        # (save data to file IF {saveTXT} is True
532        if self.pars.saveTXT is True:
533            bground.points.bfunc.save_bkg_data(self)

Run {RestoreFromPoints} method of background subtraction.

  • The method reads a TXT.BP file with background points and recalculates background and saves the results.
  • The TXT.BP file usually comes from a previous run of InteractivePlot method.
  • No params; everything defined during InteractivePlot initialization.
class SimpleFuncs: 
536class SimpleFuncs:
537    '''
538    SimpleFunc background subtraction method(s).
539    
540    * This is a specific case (and subclass) of InteractivePlot method.
541    * It inherits the initialization and visualization from InteractivePlot.
542    * But it runs non-interactively, just reading bkgpoints + calculating bkg.
543    
544    The method can be run in two ways:
545        
546    * Classical, step-by-step approach - see the example below
547    * Modern, single-funtion approach - see bground.api.Run.SimpleFunc
548    
549    Example - running SimpleFunc in classical way:
550        
551    >>> # Standard import
552    >>> # (alternative: import ediff as ed => bkg is available as ed.bkg
553    >>> import bground.api as bkg
554    >>>
555    >>> # Define I/O files
556    >>> IN  = 'data1_raw_intensity.txt'
557    >>> BKG = 'data2_bkg_corrected.txt'
558    >>>
559    >>> # Define auxiliary objects
560    >>> DATA = bkg.InputData(IN, usecols=[0,1], unpack=True)
561    >>> BPAR = bkg.BkgParams(BKG,'Pixel','Intensity',xlim=[0,200],ylim=[0,280])
562    >>>
563    >>> # Initialize and run SimpleFunc bkg subtraction method
564    >>> # here: selected SimpleFunc method = RollingBall (+ its params)
565    >>> SMET = bkg.SimpleFuncs(DATA, PPAR)
566    >>> SMET.run(algorithm='RollingBall', xrange=(40,250), radius=70)
567
568    TODO: Evard ...
569    
570    * Add simple tailored NumPy/SciPy based function
571    * This class should not be modified => add func to sfunc.sfunc
572    '''
573    
574    def __init__(self, DATA, PARS):
575        '''
576        Initialize {SimpleFunc} object.
577        
578        * The {SimpleFunc} object can run simple bkg subtraction methods.
579
580        Parameters
581        ----------
582        DATA : bground.api.InputData object
583            The object contains description of input data.
584            The {InputData} object should be defined
585            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
586            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
587        PARS : bground.api.BkgParams object
588            The object contains description of input data.
589            The {BkgData} object should be defined
590            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
591            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
592
593        Returns
594        -------
595        bground.api.SimpleFunc object
596            The object should be ready to use.
597            The principal object method is SimpleFunc.run(method=...).
598        '''
599        
600        # Save properties from DATA object
601        self.name = DATA.name
602        self.data = DATA.data
603        self.diff1D = DATA.diff1D
604        
605        # Save properties from PARS object
606        self.pars = PARS
607        
608        # Additional property - empty XYbackground object
609        # (this object is defined as a semi-empty object here
610        # (the only argument we supply is the name of the background file
611        self.background = \
612            bground.points.bdata.XYbackground(self.pars.bkg_file)
613                        
614        # Initialize plotting - by means of composition
615        self.plots = Plots(self)
616        
617
618    def run(self, algorithm='RollingBall', **kwargs):
619        '''
620        Run specific background subtraction {method} from {SimpleFunc} class.
621        
622        * Default SimpleFunc method is 'RollingBall'.
623        * Optional {kwargs} are passed to the selected method.
624        
625        Parameters
626        ----------
627        method : str, optional, default is 'RollingBall'
628            Name of the background subtraction method.
629            Implemented methods: 'RollingBall'.
630        kwargs : keyword arguments
631            Arbitrary keyword arguments.
632            The arguments are passed to selected bkg subtraction {method}.
633            Example: for 'RollingBall' method, we should specify {radius} arg.
634        
635        Summary of kwargs
636        -----------------
637        xrange : tuple/list of two floats
638            Argument relevant to all functions/algorithms.
639            If None, then the func/algorihtm will use the whole range
640            as defined in calling object = self.pars.xlim.
641            If specified (recommended), then the algorithm/func will use
642            the selected sub-range where are the relevant diffraction peaks.
643        radius: integer
644            Argument relevant to 'RollingBall' algorithm.
645            Radius of the rolling ball.
646        
647        Returns
648        -------
649        None
650            The results are saved in self object,
651            specifically in self.background (bkg curve)
652            and self.data (bgk-corrected data).
653            The bkg-corrected data are also saved in a TXT-file
654            if saveTXT=False argument was not used in initialization.
655        '''
656
657        # (1) Run the selected bkg subtraction method
658        # (the results are ALWAYS auto-saved in self.background and self.data
659        if algorithm == 'RollingBall':
660            bground.sfunc.sfunc.rolling_ball(self, **kwargs)
661        else:
662            raise ValueError('Uknown background subtraction method!')
663        
664        # (2) Save the calculated data ALSO in self.diff1D => if it was defined
665        # (self.diff1D IF ediff.io.Diffractogram1D used for the initialization 
666        if self.diff1D is not None:
667            self.diff1D['Ibkg'] = self.data[2]
668            self.diff1D['I']    = self.data[3]
669
670        # (3) Save the calculated data ALSO to out_file => if it was defined
671        # (save data to file IF {saveTXT} is True
672        # (we can use the existing func from bground.points sub-package
673        if self.pars.saveTXT is True:
674            bground.points.bfunc.save_bkg_data(self)
675        
676        # (4) Print a brief message if requested
677        if self.pars.messages is True:
678            print('Backgroud subtraction :: SimpleFunc/RollingBall.')
679            if self.pars.saveTXT is True:
680                print(f'Background file: {self.pars.bkg_file}')

SimpleFunc background subtraction method(s).

  • This is a specific case (and subclass) of InteractivePlot method.
  • It inherits the initialization and visualization from InteractivePlot.
  • But it runs non-interactively, just reading bkgpoints + calculating bkg.

The method can be run in two ways:

  • Classical, step-by-step approach - see the example below
  • Modern, single-funtion approach - see bground.api.Run.SimpleFunc

Example - running SimpleFunc in classical way:

>>> # Standard import
>>> # (alternative: import ediff as ed => bkg is available as ed.bkg
>>> import bground.api as bkg
>>>
>>> # Define I/O files
>>> IN  = 'data1_raw_intensity.txt'
>>> BKG = 'data2_bkg_corrected.txt'
>>>
>>> # Define auxiliary objects
>>> DATA = bkg.InputData(IN, usecols=[0,1], unpack=True)
>>> BPAR = bkg.BkgParams(BKG,'Pixel','Intensity',xlim=[0,200],ylim=[0,280])
>>>
>>> # Initialize and run SimpleFunc bkg subtraction method
>>> # here: selected SimpleFunc method = RollingBall (+ its params)
>>> SMET = bkg.SimpleFuncs(DATA, PPAR)
>>> SMET.run(algorithm='RollingBall', xrange=(40,250), radius=70)

TODO: Evard ...

  • Add simple tailored NumPy/SciPy based function
  • This class should not be modified => add func to sfunc.sfunc
SimpleFuncs(DATA, PARS) 
574    def __init__(self, DATA, PARS):
575        '''
576        Initialize {SimpleFunc} object.
577        
578        * The {SimpleFunc} object can run simple bkg subtraction methods.
579
580        Parameters
581        ----------
582        DATA : bground.api.InputData object
583            The object contains description of input data.
584            The {InputData} object should be defined
585            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
586            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
587        PARS : bground.api.BkgParams object
588            The object contains description of input data.
589            The {BkgData} object should be defined
590            either before initializing the {InteractivePlot},
591            or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
592
593        Returns
594        -------
595        bground.api.SimpleFunc object
596            The object should be ready to use.
597            The principal object method is SimpleFunc.run(method=...).
598        '''
599        
600        # Save properties from DATA object
601        self.name = DATA.name
602        self.data = DATA.data
603        self.diff1D = DATA.diff1D
604        
605        # Save properties from PARS object
606        self.pars = PARS
607        
608        # Additional property - empty XYbackground object
609        # (this object is defined as a semi-empty object here
610        # (the only argument we supply is the name of the background file
611        self.background = \
612            bground.points.bdata.XYbackground(self.pars.bkg_file)
613                        
614        # Initialize plotting - by means of composition
615        self.plots = Plots(self)

Initialize {SimpleFunc} object.

  • The {SimpleFunc} object can run simple bkg subtraction methods.
Parameters
  • DATA (bground.api.InputData object): The object contains description of input data. The {InputData} object should be defined either before initializing the {InteractivePlot}, or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
  • PARS (bground.api.BkgParams object): The object contains description of input data. The {BkgData} object should be defined either before initializing the {InteractivePlot}, or intrinsically if we use bground.api.Run.InteractivePlot.
Returns
  • bground.api.SimpleFunc object: The object should be ready to use. The principal object method is SimpleFunc.run(method=...).
def run(self, algorithm='RollingBall', **kwargs): 
618    def run(self, algorithm='RollingBall', **kwargs):
619        '''
620        Run specific background subtraction {method} from {SimpleFunc} class.
621        
622        * Default SimpleFunc method is 'RollingBall'.
623        * Optional {kwargs} are passed to the selected method.
624        
625        Parameters
626        ----------
627        method : str, optional, default is 'RollingBall'
628            Name of the background subtraction method.
629            Implemented methods: 'RollingBall'.
630        kwargs : keyword arguments
631            Arbitrary keyword arguments.
632            The arguments are passed to selected bkg subtraction {method}.
633            Example: for 'RollingBall' method, we should specify {radius} arg.
634        
635        Summary of kwargs
636        -----------------
637        xrange : tuple/list of two floats
638            Argument relevant to all functions/algorithms.
639            If None, then the func/algorihtm will use the whole range
640            as defined in calling object = self.pars.xlim.
641            If specified (recommended), then the algorithm/func will use
642            the selected sub-range where are the relevant diffraction peaks.
643        radius: integer
644            Argument relevant to 'RollingBall' algorithm.
645            Radius of the rolling ball.
646        
647        Returns
648        -------
649        None
650            The results are saved in self object,
651            specifically in self.background (bkg curve)
652            and self.data (bgk-corrected data).
653            The bkg-corrected data are also saved in a TXT-file
654            if saveTXT=False argument was not used in initialization.
655        '''
656
657        # (1) Run the selected bkg subtraction method
658        # (the results are ALWAYS auto-saved in self.background and self.data
659        if algorithm == 'RollingBall':
660            bground.sfunc.sfunc.rolling_ball(self, **kwargs)
661        else:
662            raise ValueError('Uknown background subtraction method!')
663        
664        # (2) Save the calculated data ALSO in self.diff1D => if it was defined
665        # (self.diff1D IF ediff.io.Diffractogram1D used for the initialization 
666        if self.diff1D is not None:
667            self.diff1D['Ibkg'] = self.data[2]
668            self.diff1D['I']    = self.data[3]
669
670        # (3) Save the calculated data ALSO to out_file => if it was defined
671        # (save data to file IF {saveTXT} is True
672        # (we can use the existing func from bground.points sub-package
673        if self.pars.saveTXT is True:
674            bground.points.bfunc.save_bkg_data(self)
675        
676        # (4) Print a brief message if requested
677        if self.pars.messages is True:
678            print('Backgroud subtraction :: SimpleFunc/RollingBall.')
679            if self.pars.saveTXT is True:
680                print(f'Background file: {self.pars.bkg_file}')

Run specific background subtraction {method} from {SimpleFunc} class.

  • Default SimpleFunc method is 'RollingBall'.
  • Optional {kwargs} are passed to the selected method.
Parameters
  • method (str, optional, default is 'RollingBall'): Name of the background subtraction method. Implemented methods: 'RollingBall'.
  • kwargs (keyword arguments): Arbitrary keyword arguments. The arguments are passed to selected bkg subtraction {method}. Example: for 'RollingBall' method, we should specify {radius} arg.
Summary of kwargs

xrange : tuple/list of two floats Argument relevant to all functions/algorithms. If None, then the func/algorihtm will use the whole range as defined in calling object = self.pars.xlim. If specified (recommended), then the algorithm/func will use the selected sub-range where are the relevant diffraction peaks. radius: integer Argument relevant to 'RollingBall' algorithm. Radius of the rolling ball.

Returns
  • None: The results are saved in self object, specifically in self.background (bkg curve) and self.data (bgk-corrected data). The bkg-corrected data are also saved in a TXT-file if saveTXT=False argument was not used in initialization.
class BaseLines: 
682class BaseLines:
683    '''
684    BaseLines method of backround subtraction.
685
686    This method uses algorithms from pybaselines for automatic baseline 
687    detection.
688
689    Example 1 :: algorithm with default parameters
690
691    >>> # Standard import
692    >>> import bground.api as bkg
693    >>> 
694    >>> # (1) Define input and output file
695    >>> IN  = 'ed1_raw.txt'  # input file,  2cols: X, Yraw
696    >>> OUT = 'ed2_bkg.txt'  # output file, 4cols: X, Yraw, Ybkg, Y=Yraw-Ybkg
697    >>>
698    >>> # (2) Call the method, subtract background, and save results.
699    >>> BMET = bkg.BaseLines(IN, OUT)
700    >>> BMET.run(algorithm='peak_filling', xrange=(30,250))
701
702
703    Example 2 :: specify algorithm parameters
704
705    >>> # Standard import
706    >>> import bground.api as bkg
707    >>> 
708    >>> # (1) Define input and output file
709    >>> IN  = 'ed1_raw.txt'  # input file,  2cols: X, Yraw
710    >>> OUT = 'ed2_bkg.txt'  # output file, 4cols: X, Yraw, Ybkg, Y=Yraw-Ybkg
711    >>>
712    >>> # (2) Call the method, subtract background, and save results.
713    >>> # Add parameter decreasing=True for the snip algorithm.
714    >>> BMET = bkg.BaseLines(IN, OUT)
715    >>> BMET.run(algorithm='snip', xrange=(30,250), decreasing=True)
716
717    '''
718
719    
720    def __init__(self, input_data, bkg_params):
721        '''
722        Initialize BaseLines.
723
724        Parameters
725        ----------
726        input_data : str or bground.api.InputData
727            Can be InputData directly or any type supported by InputData.
728        bkg_params : str or bground.api.BkgParams
729            Can be a string with a output path or BkgParams object.
730        '''
731        if isinstance(input_data, InputData):
732            self.data = input_data.data
733            self.diff1D = input_data.diff1D
734        else:
735            input_data = InputData(input_data)
736            self.data = input_data.data
737            self.diff1D = input_data.diff1D
738
739        if isinstance(bkg_params, BkgParams):
740            self.pars = bkg_params
741        else:
742            self.pars = BkgParams(bkg_params)
743
744        self.x, self.y = self.data
745        
746        # necessary for save_bkg_data function
747        self.background = \
748            bground.points.bdata.XYbackground(self.pars.bkg_file)
749
750        self.plots = Plots(self)
751
752    def run(self, algorithm = "peak_filling", xrange=None, **kwargs):
753        '''Run the background subtraction.
754
755        Parameters
756        ----------
757        algorithm : str, optional, by default "peak_filling"
758            Algorithm from pybaselines for baseline detection.     
759        xrange : tuple with two floats, optional, by default None
760            A range on the x axis, where the baseline should be subtracted.
761            The range is inclusive. If None, the whole range is used.
762
763        Returns
764        -------
765        None
766            The results are saved in self object,
767            specifically in self.background (bkg curve)
768            and self.data (bgk-corrected data).
769            The bkg-corrected data are also saved in a TXT-file
770            if saveTXT=False argument was not used in initialization.
771
772        Notes
773        -----
774        * `kwargs` are passed to the algorithm.
775
776        * Recommended algorithms (and parameters in kwargs):
777            * `peak_filling` (with parameter `half_window=1`),
778            * `snip` (with parameter `decreasing=True`),
779            * `pspline_iasls` (with parameter `lam=5`), 
780            * `irsqr` (with parameter `lam=1000`),
781            * `rubberband` (with parameter `lam=2`)
782        
783        * Please refer to 
784        https://pybaselines.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/Baseline.html
785        for more algorithms and their details.
786        '''
787
788        bline_x, bline_y = bground.blines.blines.calculate_baseline(
789            self.x, self.y, algorithm, xrange, **kwargs)
790        
791        self.background.curve.X, self.background.curve.Y = bline_x, bline_y
792        self.data = bground.blines.blines.subtract_baseline(
793            self.x, self.y, bline_y, xrange)
794        
795        if self.diff1D is not None:
796            self.diff1D['Ibkg'] = self.data[2]
797            self.diff1D['I']    = self.data[3]
798        
799        if self.pars.saveTXT:
800            bground.points.bfunc.save_bkg_data(self)
801
802        if self.pars.messages is True:
803            print(f'Backgroud subtraction :: BaseLines/{algorithm}.')
804            if self.pars.saveTXT is True:
805                print(f'Background file: {self.pars.bkg_file}')

BaseLines method of backround subtraction.

This method uses algorithms from pybaselines for automatic baseline detection.

Example 1 :: algorithm with default parameters

>>> # Standard import
>>> import bground.api as bkg
>>> 
>>> # (1) Define input and output file
>>> IN  = 'ed1_raw.txt'  # input file,  2cols: X, Yraw
>>> OUT = 'ed2_bkg.txt'  # output file, 4cols: X, Yraw, Ybkg, Y=Yraw-Ybkg
>>>
>>> # (2) Call the method, subtract background, and save results.
>>> BMET = bkg.BaseLines(IN, OUT)
>>> BMET.run(algorithm='peak_filling', xrange=(30,250))

Example 2 :: specify algorithm parameters

>>> # Standard import
>>> import bground.api as bkg
>>> 
>>> # (1) Define input and output file
>>> IN  = 'ed1_raw.txt'  # input file,  2cols: X, Yraw
>>> OUT = 'ed2_bkg.txt'  # output file, 4cols: X, Yraw, Ybkg, Y=Yraw-Ybkg
>>>
>>> # (2) Call the method, subtract background, and save results.
>>> # Add parameter decreasing=True for the snip algorithm.
>>> BMET = bkg.BaseLines(IN, OUT)
>>> BMET.run(algorithm='snip', xrange=(30,250), decreasing=True)
BaseLines(input_data, bkg_params) 
720    def __init__(self, input_data, bkg_params):
721        '''
722        Initialize BaseLines.
723
724        Parameters
725        ----------
726        input_data : str or bground.api.InputData
727            Can be InputData directly or any type supported by InputData.
728        bkg_params : str or bground.api.BkgParams
729            Can be a string with a output path or BkgParams object.
730        '''
731        if isinstance(input_data, InputData):
732            self.data = input_data.data
733            self.diff1D = input_data.diff1D
734        else:
735            input_data = InputData(input_data)
736            self.data = input_data.data
737            self.diff1D = input_data.diff1D
738
739        if isinstance(bkg_params, BkgParams):
740            self.pars = bkg_params
741        else:
742            self.pars = BkgParams(bkg_params)
743
744        self.x, self.y = self.data
745        
746        # necessary for save_bkg_data function
747        self.background = \
748            bground.points.bdata.XYbackground(self.pars.bkg_file)
749
750        self.plots = Plots(self)

Initialize BaseLines.

Parameters
  • input_data (str or bground.api.InputData): Can be InputData directly or any type supported by InputData.
  • bkg_params (str or bground.api.BkgParams): Can be a string with a output path or BkgParams object.
def run(self, algorithm='peak_filling', xrange=None, **kwargs): 
752    def run(self, algorithm = "peak_filling", xrange=None, **kwargs):
753        '''Run the background subtraction.
754
755        Parameters
756        ----------
757        algorithm : str, optional, by default "peak_filling"
758            Algorithm from pybaselines for baseline detection.     
759        xrange : tuple with two floats, optional, by default None
760            A range on the x axis, where the baseline should be subtracted.
761            The range is inclusive. If None, the whole range is used.
762
763        Returns
764        -------
765        None
766            The results are saved in self object,
767            specifically in self.background (bkg curve)
768            and self.data (bgk-corrected data).
769            The bkg-corrected data are also saved in a TXT-file
770            if saveTXT=False argument was not used in initialization.
771
772        Notes
773        -----
774        * `kwargs` are passed to the algorithm.
775
776        * Recommended algorithms (and parameters in kwargs):
777            * `peak_filling` (with parameter `half_window=1`),
778            * `snip` (with parameter `decreasing=True`),
779            * `pspline_iasls` (with parameter `lam=5`), 
780            * `irsqr` (with parameter `lam=1000`),
781            * `rubberband` (with parameter `lam=2`)
782        
783        * Please refer to 
784        https://pybaselines.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/Baseline.html
785        for more algorithms and their details.
786        '''
787
788        bline_x, bline_y = bground.blines.blines.calculate_baseline(
789            self.x, self.y, algorithm, xrange, **kwargs)
790        
791        self.background.curve.X, self.background.curve.Y = bline_x, bline_y
792        self.data = bground.blines.blines.subtract_baseline(
793            self.x, self.y, bline_y, xrange)
794        
795        if self.diff1D is not None:
796            self.diff1D['Ibkg'] = self.data[2]
797            self.diff1D['I']    = self.data[3]
798        
799        if self.pars.saveTXT:
800            bground.points.bfunc.save_bkg_data(self)
801
802        if self.pars.messages is True:
803            print(f'Backgroud subtraction :: BaseLines/{algorithm}.')
804            if self.pars.saveTXT is True:
805                print(f'Background file: {self.pars.bkg_file}')

Run the background subtraction.

Parameters
  • algorithm (str, optional, by default "peak_filling"): Algorithm from pybaselines for baseline detection.
  • xrange (tuple with two floats, optional, by default None): A range on the x axis, where the baseline should be subtracted. The range is inclusive. If None, the whole range is used.
Returns
  • None: The results are saved in self object, specifically in self.background (bkg curve) and self.data (bgk-corrected data). The bkg-corrected data are also saved in a TXT-file if saveTXT=False argument was not used in initialization.
Notes

  • kwargs are passed to the algorithm.

  • Recommended algorithms (and parameters in kwargs):

    • peak_filling (with parameter half_window=1),
    • snip (with parameter decreasing=True),
    • pspline_iasls (with parameter lam=5),
    • irsqr (with parameter lam=1000),
    • rubberband (with parameter lam=2)

  • Please refer to https://pybaselines.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/Baseline.html for more algorithms and their details.

class Wavelets: 
809class Wavelets:
810    '''
811    TODO: Adri ...
812    
813    * Implement a simple wavelet method - template: SimpleFuncs - RollingBall
814    * Then implement additional method(s) according to {Cotret_2017.pdf}
815    '''

TODO: Adri ...

  • Implement a simple wavelet method - template: SimpleFuncs - RollingBall
  • Then implement additional method(s) according to {Cotret_2017.pdf}
class Run: 
 818class Run:
 819    '''
 820    The class with funcs to run each bkg subtraction method in one step.
 821    
 822    * Funcs in this class initialize and run
 823      a selected method in one step.
 824    * They set bground.api.InputData and bground.api.BkgParams
 825      and run the method.
 826    * All parameters are set in one place,
 827      which is convenient for the modern/simple OO-processing.
 828    
 829    List of the available methods:
 830    
 831    * Run.InteractivePlot   = run {InteractivePlot} bkg subtraction method
 832    * Run.RestoreFromPoints = run {RestoreFromPoints} bkg subtration method
 833    * Run.SimpleFuncs       = run {SimpleFuncs} bkg subtraction method(s)
 834    * Run.BaseLines         = run {BaseLines} bkg subtraction method(s)
 835    * Run.Wavelets          = run {Wavelets} bkg subtraction method(s)    
 836    '''
 837
 838    def InteractivePlot(
 839            in_data, bkg_file=None,
 840            comment='#', skiprows=0, header='infer', sep=r'\s+', usecols=[0,1], 
 841            xlabel=None, ylabel=None, xlim=None, ylim=None, 
 842            saveTXT=True, messages=True, CLI=False):
 843        '''
 844        Run bground.api.InteractivePlot method with a single function/command.
 845               
 846        Parameters
 847        ----------
 848        in_data : filename or np.ndarray or pd.DataFrame or ELD profile
 849            Input XYdata, containing 2 columns:
 850            `[X, Y = Iraw = raw intensity]`.
 851        bkg_file : filename, optional, default is None
 852            Name of the background/output file(s).
 853            There can be three different output files:
 854            data file ({bkg_file}.txt),
 855            background points ({bkg_file}.txt.bp),
 856            background plot ({bkg_file}.txt.png).
 857            If {bkg_file} is not specified (None) and {saveTXT}=False,
 858            then the TXT-files are not created and the results are
 859            saved only within the InteractivePlot object
 860            (in self.data, self.background, and possibly self.diff1D).
 861            The self.diff1D sub-object is saved only if the input is
 862            a ediff.io.Diffractogram1D object.
 863        comment, skiprows, header, sep, usecols: params for pd.read_csv func
 864             Parameters that are passed to pd.read_csv function
 865             if the {in_data} is an XYfile with two columns.
 866             See bground.api.InputData docs for more details.
 867         xlabel, ylabel, xlim, ylim: params for plotting
 868             Parameters that are used when plotting the XYdata
 869             in the form of matplotlib interactive graph.
 870             See bground.api.BkgParams docs for more details.
 871        saveTXT : bool, optional, default is True
 872            If True (default), save results in both TXT-file and self.diff1D.
 873            It can be switched to False in certain automated processing paths.            
 874        messages : bool, optional, default is True
 875            If True (default), print short messages on stdout.
 876        CLI : bool, optional, default is False
 877            Must be set to True when program runs from pure Python in CLI.
 878            It ensures that the interactive plot will not close immediately.
 879        
 880        Returns
 881        -------
 882        bground.api.InteractivePlot
 883            The object is used to run the InteractivePlot method,
 884            but it also saves and plots the original and bkg-corrected data.
 885        
 886        Example
 887        -------
 888        
 889        >>> # Standard import
 890        >>> # (alternative: import ediff as ed => bkg is available as ed.bkg
 891        >>> import bground.api as bkg
 892        >>>
 893        >>> # Define I/O files
 894        >>> # (input file  = XYdata, 2 cols: [X, Y = Iraw = Raw Intensity]
 895        >>> # (output file = XYdata, 4 cols: [X, Iraw, Inet, I = (Iraw - Inet)]
 896        >>> IN  = 'data1_raw_intensity.txt'
 897        >>> OUT = 'data2_bkg_subtracted.txt'
 898        >>>
 899        >>> # Run InteractivePlot subtraction method with a single function
 900        >>> # (the func initializes all objects and runs the method
 901        >>> # (SMET.data + SMET.background sub-objects will contain the results
 902        >>> SMET = bkg.Run.InteractivePlot(IN, OUT, 
 903        >>>     xlabel='Pixels', ylabel='Intensity', xlim=300, ylim=300)
 904        '''
 905        
 906        # (1) Define objects with input and output data
 907        DATA = InputData(in_data, sep=sep, usecols=usecols,
 908            comment=comment, skiprows=skiprows, header=header)
 909        PARS = BkgParams(bkg_file, 
 910            xlabel=xlabel, ylabel=ylabel, xlim=xlim, ylim=ylim,
 911            messages=messages, saveTXT=saveTXT)
 912        
 913        # (2) Define the method
 914        # (including optional argument CLI if it runs from pure CLI python
 915        SMET = InteractivePlot(DATA, PARS, CLI=CLI)
 916        
 917        # (3) Run the method
 918        SMET.run()
 919        
 920        # (4) Return the final InteractivePlot object
 921        # (the data are auto-saved to bkg_file(s)
 922        # (BUT returning the object is useful to see/show/plot the data
 923        return(SMET)
 924        
 925    
 926    def RestoreFromPoints(in_data, bkg_file, saveTXT=True,
 927            comment='#', skiprows=0, header='infer', sep=r'\s+', usecols=[0,1], 
 928            xlabel=None, ylabel=None, xlim=None, ylim=None, messages=False):
 929        '''
 930        Run bground.api.RestoreFromPoints method with a single func/command.
 931
 932        Parameters
 933        ----------
 934        in_data : filename or np.ndarray or pd.DataFrame or ELD profile
 935            Input XYdata, containing 2 columns:
 936            `[X, Y = Iraw = raw intensity]`.
 937        bkg_file : filename
 938            Name of the file with background points.
 939            According to bground package convention,
 940            the filename should be `something.txt` or `something.txt.bp`.
 941            TXT-files contain the XY-data
 942            and TXT.BP-files contain background points.
 943            If {bkg_file} is a TXT file, the extension is converted to TXT.BP.
 944        saveTXT : bool, optional, default is True
 945            If True (default), save results in both TXT-file and self.diff1D.
 946            It can be switched to False in certain automated processing paths.            
 947        comment, skiprows, header, sep, usecols: params for pd.read_csv func
 948            Parameters that are passed to pd.read_csv function
 949            if the {in_data} is an XYfile with two columns.
 950            See bground.api.InputData docs for more details.
 951         xlabel, ylabel, xlim, ylim, messages, CLI: params for plotting
 952            Parameters that are used when plotting the XYdata
 953            in the form of matplotlib interactive graph.
 954            See bground.api.BkgParams docs for more details.
 955        
 956        Returns
 957        -------
 958        bground.api.RestoreFromPoints
 959            The object is used to run the RestoreFromPoints method,
 960            but it also saves and plots the original and bkg-corrected data.
 961        
 962        Example
 963        -------
 964        
 965        >>> # Standard import
 966        >>> # (alternative: import ediff as ed => bkg is available as ed.bkg
 967        >>> import bground.api as bkg
 968        >>> 
 969        >>> # Define I/O files
 970        >>> IN  = r'orig_data.txt'
 971        >>> BKG = r'bkg-corrected_data.txt'
 972        >>> 
 973        >>> # Run RestoreFromPoints using a single command
 974        >>> SMET = bkg.Run.RestoreFromPoints(IN, BKG,
 975        >>>         xlabel='Pix', ylabel='Intensity',xlim=[0,200],ylim=180)
 976        '''
 977        
 978        # (1) Define objects with input and output data
 979        DATA = InputData(in_data, sep=sep, usecols=usecols,
 980            comment=comment, skiprows=skiprows, header=header)
 981        PARS = BkgParams(bkg_file, 
 982             xlabel=xlabel, ylabel=ylabel, xlim=xlim, ylim=ylim,
 983             saveTXT=saveTXT, messages=messages)
 984        
 985        # (2) Define the method
 986        # (including optional {out_file} to specify 
 987        SMET = RestoreFromPoints(DATA, PARS, CLI=False)
 988        
 989        # (3) Run the method
 990        SMET.run()
 991        
 992        # (4) Return the final RestoreFromPoints object
 993        # (the data are auto-saved to bkg_file(s)
 994        # (BUT returning the object is useful to see/show/plot the data
 995        return(SMET)
 996    
 997    
 998    def SimpleFuncs(in_data, bkg_file=None, saveTXT=True,
 999            comment='#', skiprows=0, header='infer', sep=r'\s+', usecols=[0,1], 
1000            xlabel=None, ylabel=None, xlim=None, ylim=None, messages=False,
1001            algorithm='RollingBall', **kwargs):
